Proposed: Alternative Solution - A 14-storey Academic Tower is proposed for the University of Toronto. Based on the height of the tower, the 2012 Ontario Building Code (OBC) requires the building to be of noncombustible construction. The building is proposed to be of mass timber construction. The following mitigating features are proposed to ensure that the Academic Tower design provides a level of performance equal to or greater than that provided by the acceptable solution in the OBC:1) Sprinkler system increased reliability:  -  Increase in hazard level used for design of sprinkler system, resulting in 50% greater water delivered, to increase           the reliability that the system will control the fire.  -  Two connections to the city water main to increase the reliability of the water supply to the sprinkler system.  -  Secondary fire pump as back-up in case the fire pump fails or is inoperable due to maintenance.  -  Second riser supplying each floor to provide redundancy in the supply of water to the location of the fire.2) Smoke control:  -  Pressurization of exit stairs in event of fire, to prevent smoke from affecting evacuation or fire department access to       the fire floor.  -  Pressurization of fire service elevator (Elevator 3) in event of fire to prevent smoke from affecting access to the             upper floors of the tower.3) Fire Resistance and Encapsulation:  -  The glued-laminated timber (glulam) beams, columns and braces that make up the exit cores (exit stair shafts) will be encapsulated in three layers of 15.9-mm-thick Type X gypsum board, to ensure the structure and stair shafts will not be adversely affected in the event of a fire. This will result in a fire-resistance rating of 2 hours provided by the gypsum board alone, with the inherent fire resistance to levels from 2.8 hours to as much as 4.8 h, resulting in much greater performance than required by the OBC acceptable solution. - The roof assembly will have a 1-h fire-resistance rating to reduce the likelihood of fire spread via the roof                        assembly.

Water closets in barrier free washrooms are with a water tank. The water closets are the elongated type, needed to facilitate user needs for the building. The water closet is 833mm high measured from the finished floor level to the top of the water tank. The OBC requires the grab bar installed behind the water closet to be located within 840mm to 920mm above the finished floor, and 150mm above the water tank. In installing the grab bar at 150mm above the water tank, the height of the grab bar will be at 983mm above the floor, exceeding the OBC height restriction by approximately 63mm. It is proposed to follow provisions in the NBC 2015 and the CSA B651-18 in mounting the grab bar at 100mm above the water tank, which will place the grab bar at 933mm above the finished floor.

Alternative Solution for Exiting and Barrier free Access:ExitingThe minimum clear width required for exterior passageway is required to be 1100 mm and OBC 3.4.1.2.(2) highlights that exits are permitted to converge provided the cumulative capacity of converging exits does not contribute more than 50% of the total required exit width for the floor area. Although an existing public exterior passageway is available on site which is shared by adjoining building, it is proposed to assess the available clear width of the existing exterior passageway within the property line through an OBC analysis. The convergence of the proposed new exit stairs is addressed with a timed-exit analysis comparing the RSET times of available travel distance from the farthest exit discharge door and OBC acceptable travel distance to reach a public thoroughfare.Barrier Free Access:The exiting primary entrance to the building is not designed as barrier free.In accordance with compliance alternative DE74 of OBC Table 11.5.1.1.D/E. existing non-barrier free principal entrance is acceptable provided at least one barrier free entrance is available. A new barrier free entrance is proposed to be provided in form of elevator lobby. The elevator lobby is accessible through an exterior passageway forming part of the right of way which is more than 1100 mm in clear width. However, clear width of the exterior passageway is varying with a pinch point of 841 mm. IT is proposed to address the minimum clearance required for a barrier free path of travel in accordance with minimum dimensions of pathway required per CSA B651 Section 4.1(a).

This Alternative Solution Proposal (ASP) summarizes the intended concepts to address the provisions of the 2012 Ontario Building Code (OBC) related to the 324-332 Richmond Street West project, located at 324-332 Richmond Street West in Toronto, Ontario. This project consists of a 27 storey mixed use project including 6 below grade levels, with a retail unit located on the Ground Floor. The P1 Level comprises of Bike Storage, Mechanical and Electrical rooms, Elevator Lobbies, Pet Grooming room and three parking stalls. The P1 Level is provided with two exit facility; Exit Stair 01 located at the northwest quadrant of the floor area and an overhead door provided where the vehicle ramp terminates at grade.  As such requirements of Sentence 3.4.2.1.(1) are satisfied. However, the overhead door does not swing on its vertical axis and as such does not meet the requirements of the OBC Clause 3.4.6.12.(1)(b).This alternative solution illustrates the compensating features proposed to be implemented in the overhead garage door design to provide an adequate level of safety.

Alterative Solution - the project consists of the construction of three houses located at 346, 348, and 350 Lee Avenue. The following outlines the proposed alternative solution for the applicable Building Code requirements, relative to fire protection and life safety, for the Finch-Kennedy SmartTrack Station of the GO Expansion program, located in Toronto, ON.Specifically, the purpose of this report is to exempt the interconnected floor spaces within the platform access portion of the Southeast, Southwest and Northwest Station Pavilion buildings from the requirement of Sentence 3.2.8.2.(6) to provide automatic sprinkler protection. It will be demonstrated that the level of life safety and fire protection provided for the Project will meet or exceed the intent of the applicable code requirements of the Ontario Building Code.The portion of the Platform Level (first storey) of the Southeast, Southwest and Northwest Station Pavilion buildings that will be superimposed above and open to the Street Level (basement level) of the buildings will be limited to stair and elevator access landings that are being considered as a contiguous volume to the Street Level of these buildings.For additional information, refer to the Interconnected Floor Space Alternative Solution Report prepared by LMDG Building Code Consultants Ltd., dated May 28, 2024.

As part of the scope of renovation, it is proposed to construct a new stair lift within the north exit stair between the ground floor and the 1st floor level. Additionally, a new ramp will also be constructed within the corridor of the ground floor level. Exit stairs are required to be designed with widths in accordance with OBC Article 3.4.3.2. and any reductions to exit width are required to conform to the provisions identified under OBC Article 3.4.3.4. Additionally, an unsprinklered building is required to be provided with a protected barrier-free path of travel in accordance with OBC Sentence 3.3.1.7.(1). It has been requested to address the exit width conditions when the stair lift is in operation (i.e. with reduced stair width) and to address the protection of a floor area provided with a new barrier-free path of travel.This alternative solution is intended to demonstrate how the proposed stair lift will maintain an equivalent level of performance as required by the Objectives and Functional Statements associated with OBC Article 3.4.3.4. and OBC Sentence 3.3.1.7.(1).
